claim_verdicts.slide: What AI assistants do well (green zone)
claim_verdicts.bullet: Drafting, summarising, restructuring, and tone-shifting text are high-reliability tasks for current chat tools
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Summaries generated by the LLMs consistently outperform both human and summaries generated by fine-tuned models across all tasks. Human-written reference summaries exhibit either an equal or higher number of hallucinations compared to GPT-4 summaries.
claim_verdicts.issue: The source (Pu et al., 'Summarization is (Almost) Dead') only evaluates summarization across five sub-tasks (single-news, multi-news, dialogue, source code, cross-lingual). The bullet extends the claim to 'drafting, restructuring, and tone-shifting' -- none of which are studied in the cited source. This is a scope mismatch: one capability (summarization) is being cited as evidence for four capabilities.
claim_verdicts.suggested_fix: Summarising text is a high-reliability task for current chat tools [claim:llm_productivity:001]; drafting, restructuring, and tone-shifting are also strong but are supported by practitioner experience rather than this study

claim_verdicts.slide: What AI assistants do well (green zone)
claim_verdicts.bullet: AI-generated summaries consistently match or beat human-written ones across news, dialogue, and code tasks
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Summaries generated by the LLMs consistently outperform both human and summaries generated by fine-tuned models across all tasks. Human-written reference summaries exhibit either an equal or higher number of hallucinations compared to GPT-4 summaries.

claim_verdicts.bullet: Arithmetic inside word problems degrades: error rates jump up to 14 percentage points as numbers get harder
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Our experiments with various models reveal a significant increase in logical error rates -- up to 14 percentage points -- as numerical complexity rises, demonstrating a general weakness in reasoning with out-of-distribution numerical values. Moreover, while models demonstrate high accuracy on standalone arithmetic tasks, their performance deteriorates substantially when computations are embedded within word problems.
claim_verdicts.issue: The source specifies 'logical error rates' (a specific category the paper distinguishes from non-logical errors such as arithmetic mistakes and number-copy errors). The bullet drops the 'logical' qualifier and says simply 'error rates', which overstates the scope of what was measured. The 14-percentage-point figure applies only to logical errors, not to total error rates.
claim_verdicts.suggested_fix: Arithmetic inside word problems degrades: logical error rates jump up to 14 percentage points as numerical complexity rises [claim:llm_productivity:002]

claim_verdicts.bullet: Chat tools fabricate citations at high rates — even the best models invent 18-29% of references
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: GhostCite benchmarks 13 LLMs across 40 domains (375K citations) with hallucination rates from 14% to 95%. ChatGPT-3.5 hallucinates 39.6% to 55% of citations in literature reviews, while GPT-4 still produces 18%-28.6% fabricated citations.

claim_verdicts.bullet: Sensitive-data leakage is the #2 risk in the OWASP Top 10 for AI apps (2025) — consumer tools may train on what you paste
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Sensitive Information Disclosure rose from #6 (2023 edition) to #2 (2025 edition) due to growing attack techniques and real-world incidents. Consumer-plan (Free, Plus) inputs may be used to train models unless users explicitly opt out via the privacy portal.

claim_verdicts.bullet: Samsung banned ChatGPT after engineers pasted source code, meeting notes, and chip-test data in three incidents within 20 days
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Samsung Electronics banned employee use of popular generative AI tools like ChatGPT after discovering staff uploaded sensitive code to the platform. Within less than 20 days of allowing ChatGPT access, Samsung experienced three separate incidents where engineers exposed confidential company information: (1) source code from a faulty semiconductor database, (2) an entire meeting transcript, (3) a test sequence for identifying yield and defective chips.

summary: 6 claims checked in this batch. 4 verified, 2 misrepresented (1 MAJOR, 1 MINOR), 0 unverified.
